title: campanula latifolia subsp. latifolia
intro: Campanula latifolia subsp. latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved bellflower, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, western Asia, and northwest Africa. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing digestive problems, and reducing fever. Campanula latifolia subsp. latifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other minor health complaints.
